meaning: n.人民党主义,旧俄民粹主义
examples: There is more than a dash of populism behind Ms Reding's attack on the industry.
Yet Britain is guilty of populism too, says a diplomat from a normally supportive country.
This new trend, dubbed "Tech Populism" by Forrester Research, is referring to the new user base of digitally savvy workers.
He is unwavering in his embrace of connoisseurship over short-attention-span novelties and trendy populism.
Not the angry populism of John Edwards, of course, but the sunny populism of Ronald Reagan.
